name: Montañas Vacías - Alto Tajo Circuit
name_original: Parque Natural del Alto Tajo – circular por ruta Montañas Vacías
description: Moderate 28.2-mile mountain bike route in Alto Tajo Natural Park, featuring canyons, the Tajo River, and remote landscapes.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Zaorejas are primarily located within or adjacent to the Alto Tajo Natural Park, a region known for its dramatic landscapes. The area is characterized by impressive canyons carved by the Tajo River, offering varied terrain for cycling. This remote and wild environment provides a sense of wilderness, making it suitable for adventurers seeking less-traveled paths. The region is also part of the "Montañas Vacías" project, highlighting its suitability for multi-day cycling adventures. Best mountain bike trails around Zaorejas The most popular mountain bike trail is Montañas Vacías and Alto Tajo Loop, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the remote landscapes of the Alto Tajo region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montañas Vacías - Alto Tajo Circuit, a moderate 28.2 miles (45.3 km) path. This circuit offers extensive views across the canyons and along the Tajo River. Local mountain bikers also love the La Hoz Somera and Los Castillejos - circular from Villanueva de Alcorón via Valsalobre, a 30.8 miles (49.6 km) trail leading through diverse local landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Mountain biking around Zaorejas is defined by deep canyons, the Tajo River, and remote natural park terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The routes in Zaorejas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Zaorejas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails are available around Zaorejas?
A: The Zaorejas area, situated within or adjacent to the Alto Tajo Natural Park, offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes, characterized by impressive canyons, the Tajo River, and remote natural park terrain. The region is known for its sense of wilderness, making it ideal for adventurers seeking less-traveled paths.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Zaorejas?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Zaorejas offers several difficult mountain bike trails. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14887130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Taravilla – Trail along the Tajo River loop from Peñalén&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 72.7 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River – Suspension bridge over the Tajo River loop from Zaorejas&lt;/a&gt;, a 92.9 km trail with significant climbs.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Zaorejas?
A: While the Zaorejas area is known for its moderate to difficult mountain bike trails, there are no routes explicitly classified as 'easy' in the immediate vicinity. The terrain, with its canyons and remote nature, generally requires a good level of fitness. However, some moderate routes might be manageable for fit beginners, especially if using an e-bike, which is a popular way to explore the park's extensive terrain.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Zaorejas?
A: The trails around Zaorejas offer stunning natural attractions, primarily within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You can expect to see dramatic canyons carved by the Tajo River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful stretch between the cliffs&lt;/a&gt;. There are also notable bridges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5331775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Peñalén&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo River Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes follow the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5373777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views of the river itself.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Zaorejas?
A: The mountain biking experience in Zaorejas is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 9 reviews. Riders frequently praise the remote and wild landscapes, the impressive canyons, and the serene Tajo River. The sense of wilderness and the opportunity to explore less-traveled paths are often highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Zaorejas?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular mountain bike routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004930583/la-hoz-somera-and-los-castillejos-circular-from-villanueva-de-alcoron-via-valsalobre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hoz Somera and Los Castillejos - circular from Villanueva de Alcorón via Valsalobre&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 49.6 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004709933/montanas-vacias-alto-tajo-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías - Alto Tajo Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 45.3 km loop offering extensive views.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in the Alto Tajo Natural Park?
A: The Alto Tajo Natural Park, including the Zaorejas area, is best explored during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, which are ideal for cycling, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and some trails might be less accessible in winter due to weather conditions.

Q: Is Zaorejas suitable for multi-day mountain biking adventures?
A: Absolutely. The Alto Tajo region, where Zaorejas is located, is part of the renowned "Montañas Vacías" (Empty Mountains) project. This initiative highlights the area's suitability for multi-day bikepacking adventures, emphasizing its wild and remote nature. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004722315/montanas-vacias-and-alto-tajo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías and Alto Tajo Loop&lt;/a&gt; are excellent starting points for exploring the region over multiple days.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Zaorejas?
A: Zaorejas is a small, remote village, but it serves as a gateway to the Alto Tajo Natural Park. While specific cafes or pubs directly on the trails are unlikely, the village itself and other nearby towns within the park may offer accommodation and dining options. It's advisable to check local listings or contact the Zaorejas tourist information center for current amenities before your visit, especially given the remote nature of the area.

Q: How can I get to Zaorejas for mountain biking, and is there parking?
A: Zaorejas is located in a remote part of Spain, within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Public transport options are limited, so driving is generally the most practical way to reach the area. Once in Zaorejas, you can typically find parking within the village. For specific trailheads, parking availability may vary, so it's recommended to check route details on komoot or local information for designated access points.

Q: Are e-bikes a good option for exploring the mountain bike trails around Zaorejas?
A: Yes, e-bikes are an excellent option for exploring the extensive and often challenging terrain around Zaorejas and within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. They allow more visitors to enjoy the 'hidden paths' and cover greater distances, making the moderate and difficult routes more accessible. Some services in the region even offer e-bike rentals.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular route in the Alto Tajo Natural Park is considered moderate. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. Due to the elevation gain, the toughness of the terrain, and the remoteness of the area, it is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of forest roads and double tracks, intentionally avoiding asphalt. While generally technically easy gravel roads, some points might be challenging even for well-equipped bikes. Riders with lighter gravel tires might encounter short hike-a-bike sections.

faqs.question: What kind of bike and tires are recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: A mountain bike is ideal for this route. If you're using a gravel bike, a tire width of around 40-42mm is recommended to handle the varied gravel surfaces and potential challenging sections.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The starting point for this circular route is typically within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Specific parking information would depend on your chosen access point within the park, but generally, you can find parking in the small villages or designated areas near trailheads.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for the Alto Tajo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required for cycling within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and private land.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning natural beauty, including deep canyons, the crystal-clear turquoise waters of the Tajo River, and extensive forests. The landscape has been compared to parts of the American West or Chilean Patagonia. You might spot diverse wildlife such as otters, roe deer, and foxes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the San Pedro Bridge, a scenic Trail along the Tajo River, and the La Atalaya Shelter. The route also passes through many small, often depopulated villages, offering a glimpse into the 'Spanish Lapland' concept.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters can bring cold weather and snow, making some sections impassable.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Alto Tajo Natural Park, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the remote nature of the area and potential encounters with other animals.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is part of the larger 'Montañas Vacías' project and intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of the MontañasVacías route (50%) and the Camino Natural del Tajo (26%), along with smaller sections of the Camino de la Fuenta de la Parra and Camino del Pozuelo.

faqs.question: Are there areas with limited mobile phone coverage on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, be prepared for significant stretches without mobile phone coverage. The Alto Tajo Natural Park is known for its remote and wild character, meaning communication can be unreliable. It's advisable to carry a map, GPS device, and inform someone of your route and expected return time.
